title: phyteuma orbiculare subsp. tenerum
intro: Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. tenerum, commonly known as Alpine Phyteuma, is a plant species that belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties that may aid in wound healing, and help in the treatment of various infections. The therapeutic actions of Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. tenerum include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect the body from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

side_effects: Phyteuma orbiculare subsp. tenerum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals.
